mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS from its recent elevated level signals easing financial conditions, lowers discount rates, and lifts equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days turn positive after rate-cut momentum is established.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls at least 25 bps from its 3-month high
failure_condition: If triggered instances do not show higher 20d forward returns than untriggered periods, or if the hit rate is below 50% over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE reflects weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month when labor deterioration becomes visible in the monthly release.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20d S&P 500 returns more often than negative returns, or if average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean across any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is accelerating while HOUST is not, retail demand is firm enough to support revenue growth without a housing-led tightening shock, and equities should rise over the next 5 trading days as cyclical sales expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and flat-to-falling HOUST does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the interaction signal has negative average 5d returns in a rolling 18-month test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in HOUST signals housing demand collapse and future construction and consumption weakness, causing S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 8% from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by below-baseline 20d equity returns, or if the triggered sign accuracy falls below 45% over any 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO indicates stronger current production and better utilization of industrial capacity, which raises near-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO gains fail to predict positive 20d returns relative to baseline, or if the signal becomes negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a fall in UNRATE identifies broad labor-market improvement, which should boost consumption and corporate sales expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-improvement condition does not outperform PAYEMS alone or UNRATE alone, or if 60d forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, a flat or rising CPIAUCSL accompanied by unchanged FEDFUNDS implies real rates are falling, which eases financial conditions and supports a positive S&P 500 response over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS combination does not outperform either series separately in high-inflation periods, or if triggered 5d returns are not above the unconditional mean over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is weak but retail sales remain resilient, markets infer that consumption is cushioning the slowdown, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days as recession risk is repriced downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below its prior-year pace while RSAFS is positive year over year
failure_condition: Reject if weak-GDP/firm-retail regimes do not yield positive 60d forward returns, or if the interaction signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.
